title: Help, Result Formatting, Streams, and Terminal Accessibility · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs help, result formatting, streams, and terminal accessibility from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for screen-reader-friendly deterministic terminal presenter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Generate concise overview and per-command help from one registry while showing syntax, examples, defaults, and exit paths.
Assign data, diagnostics, progress, and usage to deliberate streams and return stable command outcomes or exit statuses.
Sort maps and sets, label units, preserve stable field order, and distinguish absent, zero, empty, and unknown values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
